search_query: idiom for getting familiar at work
combined_text: Learn the Ropes < All Phrases Learn the Ropes Sep 4, 2014Learn the ropes means to learn how to do something. Example of use: “ Once I learn the ropes at my new job, I won’t be so nervous and I’ll be very good at the work I do.”Interesting fact Not surprisingly, the origin of the idiom “learn the ropes” is nautical.
